verdict.answer_first: B states the diagnostic move immediately: 'open the browser Network tab, reproduce, and count the outgoing requests'; A starts with 'Good news' before the answer.
verdict.decisiveness: B commits to one first move, 'count the outgoing requests'; A is useful but spreads attention across a longer 'Work it in this order' checklist.
verdict.fabrication: A has more unsupported precision: '< ~50ms', '100–500ms', and 'Most double-submits'; B also has unsupported ranges but fewer inflated claims.
verdict.actionability: B is easier to execute now: 'Two requests leave the browser' vs 'One request leaves the browser' plus concrete checks like 'getEventListeners(formEl)'.
